analysis.reasoning: Core message: The EU marks World & European Day Against the Death Penalty and invites people to a virtual screening and discussion on capital punishment in the U.S., using hashtags #AbolishDeathPenalty and #EU4HumanRights. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story): Not about EU history or overcoming internal obstacles. EU2 (united): No mention of intra-EU unity or agreement. EU3 (useful for its citizens): Not about internal EU policies improving citizens’ lives. EU4 (avant-garde political actor): Not about pioneering regulation or future challenges broadly; focus is human rights advocacy. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Yes—explicitly positions the EU as championing human rights and abolition of the death penalty, including engagement on the issue abroad. EU6 (contributes to a better world): Implicitly yes—the EU promotes global human rights and abolition efforts, contributing to a better world through advocacy and dialogue beyond its borders. Conclusion: EU5 clearly applies; EU6 also applies.
